headline: Vehicle Sales Hit 800,000 Units But Household Debt Soars
context: MAA reported 2025 vehicle sales of 802,000 units, a record year driven by Perodua and Proton, while Bank Negara flagged that hire purchase debt reached RM310 billion — 20% of total household borrowing.

cards.big: 802,000 cars sold but RM310 billion in hire purchase debt — Malaysians are drowning in car loans to commute to work
cards.sub: Average car loan tenure is 9 years at RM850 monthly — 25% of median household income locked into vehicle payments

cards.big: Car ownership costs including loan, insurance, fuel, and maintenance average RM1,650 monthly per vehicle
cards.sub: Malaysian households spend 22% of income on transport — highest in ASEAN and double Singapore's 11%

cards.big: 82% of KL commuters drive alone — public transport modal share at 18% unchanged in a decade despite MRT investment
cards.sub: Car dependency forces B40 families into 9-year debt cycles for depreciating assets that lose 40% value in 5 years

cards.big: National Automotive Policy protects Proton and Perodua with 50-100% import duties on foreign cars
cards.sub: Protected market means Malaysians pay 40-80% more for cars than Indonesians or Thais — a hidden consumption tax

cards.big: Record car sales in a country with failing public transport isn't an economic success — it's a transport policy failure that traps families in debt
Should Malaysia phase out automotive import duties over 10 years while investing savings into free urban public transport?
